notes: Three discriminating evals are defined; no live benchmark results are claimed.

evals.prompt: A deployed Kieran's LifeTrkr page loads HTML but reports asset 404s under the GitHub Pages repository path. Diagnose the likely configuration issue and propose the smallest compatible fix.
expectations: Checks vite.config.ts and identifies the production base /kierans-lifetrkr/ versus local / behavior.
Preserves src/App.tsx HashRouter instead of proposing BrowserRouter as the fix.
Checks the workflow artifact path and the post-build dist/index.html to dist/404.html copy step.
Separates a local build result from live Pages verification and reports the exact validation steps.

evals.prompt: Review a request to add a server and gh-pages branch because this Vite SPA has a GitHub Pages subpath. Explain whether the request fits the repository and what should be changed instead.
expectations: Rejects server hosting and gh-pages branch changes as outside the client-only Actions deployment contract.
References the checked-in production base /kierans-lifetrkr/ and HashRouter constraints.
Uses .github/workflows/static.yml as the deployment source of truth, including the dist artifact and 404 fallback.
Reports the conflict, safe alternative, validation status, and any remaining live-environment unknowns.

evals.prompt: GitHub Actions fails during npm ci before the Vite build, while the workflow also normalizes Replit-internal lockfile URLs. Diagnose the safe response for this repository.
expectations: Preserves npm ci, the checked-in package-lock.json, and the workflow's public registry URL normalization.
Recognizes missing @emnapi/core or @emnapi/runtime as a dependency metadata blocker when that is the observed error.
Does not switch to pnpm, npm install, gh-pages, or a backend workaround without authorization.
Distinguishes the install blocker from build/deployment health and gives a precise report contract.
